item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll inspect the specified source files, add the shipments collection and matching data-access module, then run the test suite against the configured live database.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/orders.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && printf '\\n--- package ---\\n' && sed -n '1,160p' package.json"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/orders.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && printf '\\n--- package ---\\n' && sed -n '1,160p' package.json"

item.text: The new module follows the existing account/order access pattern; the schema now validates all requested shipment fields, including the date and status enum. I’m running acceptance tests now.

item.text:
Implemented shipments support:

- Added validated `shipments` collection in [schema.mjs](/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-complex/new-entity/mongo/codex/repeat-3/workspace/src/schema.mjs).
- Added [shipments.mjs](/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-complex/new-entity/mongo/codex/repeat-3/workspace/src/shipments.mjs) with `createShipment`, `getShipment`, and `getShipmentsByOrder`.

Verified with `npm test`: all brownfield regression and live-database shipment acceptance checks pass.
